stories.headline: Aave goes live natively on Solana, $15B lending stack lands on the L1
stories.tldr: Aave deployed natively on Solana via Sunrise DeFi, opening its $15B global lending pool to Solana wallets and frontends.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Solana DeFi gains the largest non-EVM lending market overnight, raising the floor for SOL TVL competition with Ethereum L2s.
the_details: Trading is live across Jupiter, Backpack, Phantom, Raydium, and Meteora at launch.
Aave's pre-launch global deposits sit near $15B, with Solana now a first-class deployment target.
Move follows Solana stablecoin supply hitting $17B and RWA TVL crossing $1.85B in April.

stories.headline: Warsh Fed confirmation odds hit 97% as DOJ closes Powell probe, BTC catches a bid
stories.tldr: DOJ ended its criminal probe of Jerome Powell on April 24, lifting the last political block on Kevin Warsh's confirmation as next Fed chair.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A confirmed Warsh tilts FOMC dovish in crypto's read, pulling rate-cut odds and risk-asset positioning forward into Q3.
the_details: Prediction markets price Warsh confirmation at 97% after Pirro announced probe closure on April 24.
BTC up 18% on the month into FOMC, with $2.5B in April spot ETF inflows backstopping the bid.
Fed's inspector general now handles the $2.5B HQ renovation cost-overrun review.

stories.headline: Lightspark ships Bitcoin wallet built for AI agents under David Marcus
stories.tldr: Ex-Meta crypto chief David Marcus unveiled a Lightspark Bitcoin wallet that lets AI agents transact autonomously over Lightning.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Programmable BTC payments for autonomous agents pushes Bitcoin beyond store-of-value into machine-to-machine commerce rails.
the_details: Wallet targets agent-driven payments like API calls, content licensing, and inference settlement.
Rides Lightning Network for sub-second, sub-cent settlement at scale.
Ships as Marcus pushes Lightspark's enterprise Bitcoin and stablecoin payments business.

stories.headline: Judge denies Sam Bankman-Fried retrial bid, calls 'new evidence' baseless
stories.tldr: A US judge rejected Sam Bankman-Fried's motion for a new trial, ruling his fresh-evidence and prosecutorial-misconduct claims unsupported.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Closes a remaining legal off-ramp for SBF and locks in the FTX wind-down narrative as creditor distributions continue through 2026.
the_details: Court called the new-evidence claim 'inconsistent with facts' and dismissed prosecutor allegations as 'baseless.'
Ruling preserves SBF's 25-year sentence handed down in March 2024.
Comes as FTX estate continues multi-billion-dollar creditor payouts on schedule.

stories.headline: Tropykus halts deposits and loans, v1 users have until July 2026 to withdraw
stories.tldr: Bitcoin-denominated lending protocol Tropykus is sunsetting v1, suspending deposits and loans and urging users to pull funds before July 2026.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Another small DeFi protocol exiting underscores the long-tail consolidation as TVL concentrates into Aave, Morpho, and Sky.
the_details: All v1 deposits and loans suspended effective immediately.
Hard withdrawal deadline set for July 2026.
Team has not committed to a v2 timeline at the announcement.
